nodes.label: AI as Excel formula assistant
nodes.label_negative: Manual reliance on basic Excel knowledge
nodes.grounding_span: the rise of AI has allowed me to use more complex functions that I had never heard of before

nodes.label: AI as time-saving tool
nodes.grounding_span: There's probably a macro or something that can save me a lot of time with this

nodes.label: AI reliability for practical tasks
nodes.grounding_span: The formula returns a bunch of # symbols

nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner
nodes.grounding_span: I will happily collaborate until it is resolved

nodes.label: AI for complex tasks vs. self for simple tasks
nodes.grounding_span: If it only concerns a few rows, or is something exceedingly simple... I will do it myself

nodes.label: AI integration into existing software
nodes.label_negative: AI limited to common tools like Excel
nodes.grounding_span: I hope I could use it to discover functions in SAP the way I have been able to with Excel

nodes.label: Productivity and efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: managed to complete it after 30 minutes of trial and error with an AI

nodes.label: Professional competence
nodes.grounding_span: I have confidence in being able to articulate the situation in a way that the AI will understand

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as a helpful tool
nodes.grounding_span: It was fantastic, and I knew at that moment that AI could help me do almost anything

nodes.label: Initial self-doubt and anxiety
nodes.grounding_span: I had a slight feeling of 'Maybe my Excel is the wrong version'... anxious thoughts related to my potential incompetence

nodes.label: Resistance to AI for personal communication
nodes.grounding_span: I no longer have any doubts about whether it will get sorted out or not

nodes.label: Frustration with iterative collaboration
nodes.grounding_span: If it doesn't pan out immediately, no big deal, I will happily collaborate until it is resolved

nodes.label: Skepticism about AI for non-standardized systems
nodes.grounding_span: My 'super dumbed down' approach to explaining my objective would not work as well in such a use-case

nodes.label: Systematic decomposition and contextualization
nodes.grounding_span: I'll start from a 'zoomed out' perspective of the entire task before delving into the minutiae

nodes.label: Iterative refinement and testing
nodes.grounding_span: I will let it know 'The formula returns a bunch of # symbols' or 'Nothing happened' and we take it from there
